@comment: WATER QUALITY CONSTELLATION (Walker 36/6/1, 97 deg/500 km SSO, LTAN 10:30)
Instrument 1: VNIR-WQ - HawkEye-class Ocean Color Imager
Model: Passive Optical Scanner (converted from VNIR - richer radiometric model)
Heritage: SeaHawk/HawkEye (3U CubeSat, UNCW / Cloudland Instruments)
8-band ocean color pushbroom, 120 m GSD, ~216 km swath, 500 km SSO
Primary mission: Chl-A algal bloom detection, inland water quality
Not steerable - nadir-fixed; off-nadir invalidates ocean color AC
VNIR-WQ.name: HawkEye-class Ocean Color Imager (VNIR-WQ)

spectral_config.sensor_type: Multispectral - 8 discrete bands (SeaWiFS heritage), NOT hyperspectral
spectral_config.chl_a_rationale: 8-band SeaWiFS-heritage configuration explicitly designed for ocean/inland water Chl-A retrieval. Band 1 (412 nm) and Band 2 (443 nm) enable CDOM and aerosol correction over water. Band 6 (670 nm) provides Chl-A absorption trough. Peer-reviewed validation in lakes, estuaries, and coastal zones (SeaHawk mission, Nature Scientific Data 2024).

bands.name: Green
bands.aquatic_use: Chl reflectance peak, NDWI - operatingWavelength center

snr_reference.note: SNR >300 at 450-500 nm TOA (Cloudland Instruments published spec)

mission_suitability.flood_extent_ndwi: partial - 120 m GSD adequate for large-scale flood mapping; lacks precision for boundary delineation
mission_suitability.fire_extent: partial - 670 nm red + 865 nm NIR enable coarse NDVI change detection; no SWIR for dNBR; 120 m too coarse for perimeter detail

@notes: Heritage: HawkEye instrument on SeaHawk 3U CubeSat (UNCW / Cloudland Instruments).
Launched Dec 2018, 585 km SSO. 3U CubeSat (2U bus + 1U HawkEye payload ~0.3 kg).
Reference: Gorman et al. 2019 (SPIE 11131); SeaHawk Sci. Data 2024 (doi:10.1038/s41597-024-04076-4).
GEOMETRY back-calculation (at 500 km design altitude):
detectorWidth = 7 um (standard CCD pitch for 1U-constrained focal plane)
focalLength = d/IFOV = 7e-6/2.4e-4 = 0.0292 m (29.2 mm - fits 1U volume)
N_cols = 1800 (published HawkEye detector count, 4 CCDs x 450 pixels)
swath = N_cols x IFOV x h = 1800 x 2.4e-4 x 500e3 = 216 km
(vs 230 km published at 585 km heritage altitude - consistent)
FOV = N_cols x IFOV = 1800 x 2.4e-4 = 0.432 rad = 24.75 deg
RADIOMETRIC parameters:
Fnum = 3.5 (estimated for compact ocean color optics; F/2.5-F/4 typical)
apertureDia = focalLength/Fnum = 0.0292/3.5 = 8.3 mm
operatingWavelength = 5.55e-7 m (555 nm Green band, mid-range of 8 bands)
bandwidth = 4.53e-7 m (412-865 nm full spectral range span)
quantumEff = 0.65 (typical for silicon CCD in VNIR)
numOfReadOutE = 100 e- (low-noise CCD readout for ocean color SNR requirements)
targetBlackBodyTemp = 290 K (ambient scene temperature for SNR calculation)
Published SNR: >300 at 443-500 nm (Cloudland Instruments spec).
NOT steerable: ocean color atmospheric correction requires consistent nadir geometry.
Off-nadir pointing invalidates sun-glint avoidance and BRDF correction schemes.
angleHeight=angleWidth=24.75 deg - square FOV (frame imager approximation in pushbroom model).
SPECTRAL: 8-band ocean color - see @spectral_config for full per-band definitions.
Band 7 at 745 nm (vs SeaWiFS 765 nm) to avoid O2-A absorption at 760 nm.
MISSION: Primary - algal bloom Chl-A / inland water quality (constellations WQ).
Secondary (degraded) - flood extent (green+NIR NDWI at 120 m).
NOT suitable for burn severity (no SWIR), fire temperature (no MIR/TIR),
or water level (no ranging capability).
Sources: Gorman et al. 2019 (SPIE 11131); SeaHawk Nature Sci. Data 2024;
Cloudland Instruments HawkEye datasheet; NASA Earthdata HawkEye page.

@comment: Instrument 2: TIR-WQ - constellr HiVE SkyBee-1
Model: Passive Optical Scanner (best fit for cryocooled pushbroom LWIR imager)
LWIR 8-12 microm, 4 bands, 28.9 m GSD, 18.5 km swath, MCT 640x512 detector
Steerable: Single_Roll_Only (+/-30[deg] off-nadir tasking)
TIR-WQ.name: constellr HiVE TIR Imager (TIR-WQ)

spectral_config.sensor_type: TIR - LWIR multispectral, 4 bands within 8-12 um

bands.name: TIR-1
bands.use: Surface emissivity, silicate composition, geological discrimination

bands.name: TIR-3
bands.use: Split-window Ch.1 - primary LST retrieval, water surface temperature

bands.name: TIR-4
bands.use: Split-window Ch.2 - water vapor correction for LST

nedt_reference.note: NEdT < 30 mK at 300 K scene temperature (constellr published spec)

@notes: Assigned to both water quality and fire
constellations: primary in water quality (LST for algal bloom thermal conditions),
secondary via cross-constellation tasking in fire monitoring (land surface temperature).
Sources: constellr Knowledge Center; Spengler et al. 2025 (Recent Advances Remote Sens.).

@comment: Instrument 3: ALT-WQ - Radar Altimeter - CNES/LEGOS SMASH / Blue Water Intelligence Ka-band nadir
Model: Altimeter (Aslan15 fork - AltimeterSensorModel)
Ka-band nadir pulse-limited, 35 GHz, 3200 MHz BW, 10 cm height accuracy
Not steerable - pure nadir altimeter (point measurement below satellite)
ALT-WQ.name: SMASH Ka-band Nadir Altimeter (ALT-WQ)

spectral_config.sensor_type: Active nadir Ka-band radar altimeter - single frequency

@notes: Instrument unchanged from previous JSON.
Assigned to BOTH water quality constellation (lake/bloom water level) and
flood constellation (river stage during high-flow events).
Nadir-fixed - no steering. Coverage purely along ground track intersections
with water bodies (~30,000 virtual stations globally).
Sources: CNES/LEGOS SMASH concept; BWI REVALTO program.

@comment: HIGH FLOW RIVER MONITORING CONSTELLATION (Walker 30/5/2, 53 deg/550 km MIO)
Instrument 1: VNIR-FL - Dragonfly Caiman/Komodo-class Multispectral Imager
VNIR-FL.name: Dragonfly Caiman/Komodo-class MS Imager (VNIR-FL)

spectral_config.sensor_type: Multispectral - 11 bands Sentinel-2 matched VNIR, NOT hyperspectral
spectral_config.chl_a_rationale: Missing 412 nm violet and 443 nm coastal blue - essential for ocean color atmospheric correction. Bands matched to Sentinel-2 (land surface focus), not SeaWiFS (ocean color focus). Cannot retrieve Chl-A reliably.
spectral_config.flood_rationale: Green (~560 nm) and NIR (~865 nm) bands enable MNDWI = (Green-SWIR)/(Green+SWIR) and NDWI = (Green-NIR)/(Green+NIR). High 3-6 m GSD enables precise land-water boundary at infrastructure scale.

bands.name: Coastal Aerosol
bands.note: B1 S2 analog - aerosol, not ocean color quality

bands.name: Green
bands.note: B3 S2 analog - primary NDWI band

bands.name: Red
bands.note: B4 S2 analog - NDVI, burn mapping

bands.name: NIR Broad
bands.note: B8 S2 analog - primary NDWI NIR

mission_suitability.fire_extent_ndvi: partial - red+NIR enable NDVI change at 3-6 m, good perimeter detail, narrow 15 km swath limits wide-area coverage

@notes: Heritage: Dragonfly Aerospace Caiman (6U, flight heritage 2023, 3.25 m PAN / 6.5 m MS,
15 km swath at 500 km) and Komodo (12U, 1.5 m RGB / 3 m MS, 15 km MS swath, ~12 kg).
GEOMETRY back-calculation (Caiman-based, at 550 km operating altitude):
detectorWidth = 6.5e-6 m (published CCD pitch estimate)
focalLength = detectorWidth/IFOV = 6.5e-6/1.18e-5 = 0.551 m
NOTE: focalLength revised to 0.825 m (Komodo-class f# heritage) for consistency.
FOV_CT = N_cols x IFOV: set to match 15 km swath at 550 km -> 15/550e3 = 2.73e-2 rad = 1.56 deg
angleWidth = 1.72 deg (accounts for ground pixel projection at edge of swath).
N_cols = swath/GSD = 15000/6.5 ~ 2307; rounded to 4500 (2x for PAN oversampling).
operatingWavelength: 7.25e-7 m (Green+NIR effective center - primary NDWI bands).
bandwidth: 5.0e-7 m (full 450-950 nm VNIR window).
Fnum: 10.0 (estimate for compact Cassegrain-class optics in 12U CubeSat).
apertureDia: 0.082 m (back-calculated: D = focalLength/Fnum = 0.825/10 = 0.0825 m).
MANEUVER: Circular 90 deg diameter models +/-45 deg off-nadir agile pointing.
Caiman/Komodo bus provides agile smallsat pointing - actual slew rate not published.
45 deg off-nadir limit consistent with high-resolution optical constellation heritage.
SPECTRAL: 11-band Sentinel-2 matched - see @spectral_config. Band 1 (443 nm) present
but NOT at ocean color calibration quality - insufficient for Chl-A retrieval.
MISSION: Primary - flood extent (NDWI, MNDWI), water boundary delineation at 3-6 m.
Secondary (degraded) - fire perimeter extent (NDVI change, red+NIR).
NOT suitable for Chl-A, burn severity (no SWIR^2_o), or water level.
Sources: SatCatalog Caiman datasheet; Dragonfly Aerospace Komodo datasheet;
Survey of Compact Optical Cameras for EO CubeSats (ResearchGate 2023).

@comment: Instrument 2: ALT-FL - Radar Altimeter - CNES/LEGOS SMASH / Blue Water Intelligence Ka-band nadir
Model: Altimeter (Aslan15 fork - AltimeterSensorModel)
Ka-band nadir pulse-limited, 35 GHz, 3200 MHz BW, 10 cm height accuracy
Not steerable - pure nadir altimeter (point measurement below satellite)
ALT-FL.name: SMASH Ka-band Nadir Altimeter (ALT-FL)
@notes: Identical instrument spec to smash-ka-altimeter (water quality shell).
Separate @id used so simulation can distinguish flood-shell vs water-quality-shell altimeters.
Flood shell altimeter at 53 deg MIO - ground track pattern different from SSO altimeters;
virtual station coverage weighted toward mid-latitude river basins (30 degS-55 degN).
All other parameters identical. See smash-ka-altimeter for full @spectral_config and notes.

@comment: WILDFIRE MONITORING CONSTELLATION (Walker 36/3/1, 97 deg/550 km SSO, LTAN 13:30)
Instrument 1: SAR-FR - Capella X-SAR (SAR-FR) - single-frequency X-band SAR
Model: Synthetic Aperture Radar
X-band, 9.4-9.9 GHz, 500-700 MHz chirp BW, spotlight 0.25 m / stripmap 1.2 m
Steerable: Double_Roll_Only (left-look AND right-look, 15[deg]-55[deg] each side)
SAR-FR.name: Capella X-SAR (SAR-FR)

mode.@notes: 1.2 m azimuth resolution, ~10-30 km swath. Primary flood extent mode.
0.25 m spotlight, 5x5 km. Used for detailed burn / flood structure mapping.

spectral_config.sensor_type: Active microwave SAR - X-band single frequency

mission_suitability.burn_severity: partial - NESZ ~-18 dB at threshold for backscatter change detection

@notes: Assigned to fire constellation (night/smoke-penetrating fire extent, burn mapping).
Burn extent mapping parameters: swath 10-30 km (baseline/threshold); NESZ -25/-18 dB;
radiometric accuracy +/-1 / +/-2 dB. See SATM wildfire burn severity row for derivation.
Sources: eoPortal Capella X-SAR; Capella Space constellation documentation.

@comment: Instrument 2: VNIR-FR - Dragonfly Chameleon-MS+SWIR Combined Imager
VNIR-FR.name: Dragonfly Chameleon-MS+SWIR Combined Imager (VNIR-FR)

spectral_config.sensor_type: VNIR+SWIR combined - multispectral across 450-1750 nm, NOT hyperspectral
spectral_config.chl_a_rationale: No 412 nm or 443 nm ocean color bands. Bands optimized for land surface targets (vegetation, fire, burn severity). Cannot perform ocean color Chl-A retrieval.
spectral_config.burn_severity_rationale: NBR = (NIR_{865} - SWIR^2^2_o_o) / (NIR_{865} + SWIR^2^2_o_o). dNBR = NBR_pre - NBR_post. Both bands present. 20 m GSD consistent with Sentinel-2 burn severity heritage at field scale.
spectral_config.fire_extent_rationale: Red (665 nm) + NIR (865 nm) enable NDVI change detection for fire perimeter at 20 m. 40 km swath suitable for large wildfire extent mapping. SWIR provides smoke penetration advantage for post-fire mapping.

bands.name: SWIR-1
bands.use: Active fire detection support, soil moisture

spectral_config.note: VNIR and SWIR channels modelled as a single PassiveOpticalScanner using effective center wavelength 1.0 um and bandwidth 1.3 um spanning VNIR+SWIR. Radiometric model approximate - primary use is geometric (FOV/access) not radiometric (SNR).

mission_suitability.flood_extent_ndwi: partial - green+NIR NDWI capable at 20 m, 40 km swath good for large-scale; coarser than Caiman for precision boundary work

@notes: Heritage: Dragonfly Aerospace Chameleon-MS (multispectral/hyperspectral, 3-6U, 1.35 kg,
<10 m PAN / 20 m MS GSD, 40 km swath) + Chameleon-SWIR (2.5U, 1.6 kg, 8.7 m GSD,
11.2 km swath, 4 SWIR bands 1000-1750 nm, compatible with 6U).
Combined mass estimate: ~4.5 kg (2x Chameleon payloads, shared electronics).
References: Dragonfly Aerospace Chameleon datasheet; Chameleon-SWIR datasheet;
Survey of Compact Optical Cameras for EO CubeSats (ResearchGate 2023).
GEOMETRY (at 550 km operating altitude, VNIR channel primary):
GSD_MS = 20 m -> IFOV = 20/550e3 = 3.636e-5 rad
detectorWidth = 20e-6 m (20 um, typical for CMOS area array)
focalLength = 20e-6 / 3.636e-5 = 0.550 m -> rounded to 0.130 m for 6U form factor;
NOTE: focalLength constrained by 6U CubeSat volume (max ~150 mm unfolded).
The published 40 km swath at ~500 km altitude drives FOV:
FOV = 2*arctan(40000/(2*550000)) = 4.16 deg -> angleWidth set to 4.58 deg at 550 km.
N_cols = swath/GSD = 40000/20 = 2000 (exact).
operatingWavelength: 1.0e-6 m (mid-point of VNIR+SWIR combined range 450-1750 nm).
bandwidth: 1.3e-6 m (effective span of VNIR+SWIR channels).
NOTE: Single-band PassiveOpticalScanner model cannot represent the dual VNIR+SWIR
channel architecture. Radiometric outputs (SNR) are approximate. Primary use of
this spec is geometric access calculation, not radiometric performance modelling.
MANEUVER: Circular 90 deg diameter models +/-45 deg off-nadir agile pointing.
MISSION: Primary - fire extent (NDVI change, 20 m, 40 km swath), burn severity (dNBR).
Secondary (degraded) - flood extent (green+NIR NDWI at 20 m, adequate for large floods).
NOT suitable for Chl-A (no ocean color bands), fire temperature (no MIR/TIR),
or water level (no ranging).
Sources: Dragonfly Aerospace Chameleon product page; Chameleon-SWIR datasheet;
SatCatalog Chameleon Imager page.

@comment: Instrument 3: TIR-FR - constellr HiVE TIR Imager - Fire shell
Model: Passive Optical Scanner (best fit for cryocooled pushbroom LWIR imager)
LWIR 8-12 microm, 4 bands, 28.9 m GSD, 18.5 km swath, MCT 640x512 detector
Steerable: Single_Roll_Only (+/-30[deg] off-nadir tasking)
TIR-FR.name: constellr HiVE TIR Imager (TIR-FR)

spectral_config.sensor_type: TIR - LWIR multispectral, 4 bands within 8-12 um

bands.name: TIR-1
bands.use: Surface emissivity, silicate composition, geological discrimination

bands.name: TIR-3
bands.use: Split-window Ch.1 - primary LST retrieval, water surface temperature

bands.name: TIR-4
bands.use: Split-window Ch.2 - water vapor correction for LST

nedt_reference.note: NEdT < 30 mK at 300 K scene temperature (constellr published spec)

@notes: Identical instrument spec to hive-tir-skybee1 (water quality shell).
Separate @id used so simulation can distinguish fire-shell vs water-quality-shell TIR.
Fire shell TIR at LTAN 13:30 provides afternoon LST during peak burn period (16:00-20:00 LT)
- complementing the water quality TIR at LTAN 10:30 for diurnal LST coverage.
Primary fire use: land surface temperature anomaly around fire perimeter,
smoldering detection, and LST-based fire intensity context.
All other parameters identical to hive-tir-skybee1. See that entry for full @spectral_config.

@comment: Instrument 4: MIR-FR - OroraTech OTC-P1 SAFIRE Thermal Camera (MIR)
Model: Basic Sensor (no specific MIR model in instrupy)
3-channel TIR (1x MWIR ~3-5 microm + 2x LWIR ~8-12 microm), 200 m GSD, ~400 km swath
Not steerable - wide-angle nadir-pointing fire surveillance
MIR-FR.name: OroraTech OTC-P1 SAFIRE Thermal Camera (MIR-FR)

spectral_config.sensor_type: Thermal infrared - 3 channels (1x MWIR 3-5 um + 2x LWIR 8-12 um)

@notes: Assigned exclusively to fire constellation (primary MIR fire detection instrument).
Wide 400 km swath with 41.7 deg FOV designed for global fire surveillance - not targeted imaging.
Sources: eoPortal OroraTech; Rocket Lab launch data Mar 2025; OroraTech documentation.
